Block.Para: First Session, Forty-first Parliament,
60-61 Elizabeth II, 2011-2012
HOUSE OF COMMONS OF CANADA
BILL C-437
An Act to amend the Canada Shipping Act, 2001 (prohibition against the transportation of oil by oil tankers on Canada's Pacific North Coast)
first reading, June 19, 2012

Also available on the Parliament of Canada Web Site at the following address:
http://www.parl.gc.ca
SUMMARY

This enactment amends Part 9 of the Canada Shipping Act, 2001 to prohibit the transportation of oil by oil tankers in the areas of the sea adjacent to Canada芒聙聶s Pacific North Coast known as Queen Charlotte Sound, Hecate Strait and Dixon Entrance.

Bill_Part.RunningHead_E: Canada Shipping, 2001 (prohibition against the transportation of oil by oil tankers on Canada's Pacific North Coast)

Block.Para: 1st Session, 41st Parliament,
60-61 Elizabeth II, 2011-2012
house of commons of canada
BILL C-437
An Act to amend the Canada Shipping Act, 2001 (prohibition against the transportation of oil by oil tankers on Canada's Pacific North Coast)
Whereas Canada芒聙聶s quality of life is closely connected to the sea, and the oceans are an integral part of our environmental, social, cultural and economic fabric;
Whereas the ocean environments within Canada芒聙聶s exclusive economic zone are a world treasure, home to some of the most abundant and diverse webs of marine life on earth;
Whereas it is in the public interest of Canadians to protect the ocean芒聙聶s vital natural resources;
Whereas the coastal and inland areas adjacent to the Pacific North Coast of Canada constitute the Great Bear Rainforest, the largest intact, coastal temperate rainforest left in the world;

Whereas Canada recognizes that oceans offer significant economic and cultural resources, and opportunities for economic diversification, which benefit all Canadians, and particularly coastal communities;

Whereas the sustainable economic development of the Pacific North Coast region, and especially economic opportunity in remote communities, is highly dependent on investment and employment in sectors requiring healthy ecosystems, such as fisheries and tourism;

Whereas the transportation of oil by oil tankers in certain areas of the sea adjacent to the coast of Canada poses a risk to the marine and coastal environment, to the communities that depend on that environment and to all Canadians who share in the common heritage of the oceans;

Whereas the Pacific North Coast of Canada is presently free from the risk of an oil tanker spill, as oil tankers do not currently travel in the areas of the sea known as Queen Charlotte Sound, Hecate Strait and Dixon Entrance;
Whereas alternative routes exist for the current and projected sale and transport of Canadian oil to export markets;
And whereas the Parliament of Canada wishes to affirm in law the protection of the Pacific North Coast of Canada from oil tanker spills;
Now, therefore, Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:
1. The Canada Shipping Act, 2001 is amended by adding the following after section 189:

Para.change: 189.1 The following definitions apply in sections 189.2 and 189.3.

Block.MarginalNote:
芒聙聹gas芒聙聺
脗芦 gaz 脗禄

Para.change: 芒聙聹gas芒聙聺 means natural gas and includes all substances, other than oil, that are produced in association with natural gas.

Block.MarginalNote:
芒聙聹oil芒聙聺
脗芦 p脙漏trole 脗禄

Para.change: (a) crude petroleum, regardless of gravity, produced at a well-head in liquid form, and

(b) any other hydrocarbons, except coal and gas, including hydrocarbons that may be extracted or recovered from surface or subsurface deposits, such as deposits of oil sand, bitumen, bituminous sand or oil shale,
and includes oil that has been treated to render it suitable for transportation.

Block.MarginalNote:
芒聙聹oil tanker芒聙聺
脗芦 p脙漏trolier 脗禄

Para.change: 芒聙聹oil tanker芒聙聺 means a vessel that is constructed or adapted primarily to carry oil in a hold or tank that is part of the structure of the vessel, without any intermediate form of containment.

189.2 (1) No person shall transport oil in an oil tanker in the areas of the sea adjacent to the coast of Canada known as Queen Charlotte Sound, Hecate Strait and Dixon Entrance, as described in Schedule 4 and depicted in the map set out in Schedule 5.

(2) For greater certainty, the prohibition in subsection (1) does not apply in respect of the transportation of gasoline, aviation fuel, diesel oil or fuel oil that is intended for use in coastal and island communities in Canada.

Block.Para: 2. Subsection 191(1) of the Act is amended by striking out 芒聙聹and芒聙聺 at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Para.change: (c.1) section 189.2 (prohibition against operation of oil tanker in specified area);

Block.Para: 3. The Act is amended by adding, after Schedule 3, Schedules 4 and 5 set out in the schedule to this Act.
脗聞

Bill_Part.RunningHead_E: Canada Shipping, 2001 (prohibition against the transportation of oil by oil tankers on Canada's Pacific North Coast) 芒聙聰 Schedule

Block.Para: SCHEDULE 4
DESCRIPTION OF QUEEN CHARLOTTE SOUND, HECATE STRAIT AND DIXON ENTRANCE

Those areas of the sea adjacent to the Pacific coast of Canada, bounded by a straight line beginning at 50脗掳58.522芒聙虏N Latitude and 127脗掳28.147芒聙虏W Longitude (Westcott Point), then proceeding to 50脗掳56.512芒聙虏N Latitude and 127脗掳39.513芒聙虏W Longitude (Bright Island), then proceeding to 50脗掳56.686芒聙虏N Latitude and 127脗掳53.961芒聙虏W Lon- gitude (Secretary Point), then following the northerly shoreline of Hope Island to 50脗掳54.820芒聙虏N Latitude and 127脗掳59.842芒聙虏W Longitude (Mexicana Point), then proceeding to 50脗掳52.622芒聙虏N Latitude and 128脗掳03.181芒聙虏W Longitude (Cape Sutil), then proceeding to 50脗掳47.192芒聙虏N Latitude and 128脗掳25.862芒聙虏W Longitude (Cape Scott), then proceeding to 50脗掳39芒聙虏40芒聙鲁N Latitude and 128脗掳22芒聙虏00芒聙鲁W Lon- gitude (Winifred Island), then proceeding to 50脗掳47芒聙虏03芒聙鲁N Latitude and 128脗掳46芒聙虏04芒聙鲁W Longitude (Beresford Islands), then proceeding to 50脗掳49芒聙虏02芒聙鲁N Latitude and 128脗掳56芒聙虏24芒聙鲁W Longitude (Sartine Islands), then proceeding to 50脗掳51芒聙虏09芒聙鲁N Latitude and 129脗掳05芒聙虏24芒聙鲁W Lon- gitude (Triangle Island), then proceeding to 51脗掳54芒聙虏20芒聙鲁N Latitude and 130脗掳58芒聙虏12芒聙鲁W Longitude (Kerouard Islands), then proceeding to 51脗掳54芒聙虏38芒聙鲁N Latitude and 130脗掳59芒聙虏04芒聙鲁W Longitude (Kerouard Islands), then proceeding to 51脗掳55芒聙虏20芒聙鲁N Latitude and 131脗掳00芒聙虏17芒聙鲁W Longitude (Kerouard Islands), then proceeding to 51脗掳56芒聙虏37芒聙鲁N Latitude and 131脗掳01芒聙虏52芒聙鲁W Longitude (Kunghit Island), then proceeding northerly along the west coast of the islands of Haida Gwaii to 54脗掳14芒聙虏58芒聙鲁N Latitude and 133脗掳04芒聙虏48芒聙鲁W Longitude (Langara Island), then proceeding to 54脗掳39芒聙虏45.16芒聙鲁N Latitude and 132脗掳40芒聙虏57.18芒聙鲁W Longitude (Point A of Line A-B), then following the international boundary easterly through Pearse Canal and Portland Canal to the point of intersection of the international boundary with the shoreline at the northern end of Portland Canal, as depicted in the map set out in Schedule 5.
脗聞
SCHEDULE 5
(Subsection 189.2(1))
Queen Charlotte Sound, Hecate Strait and Dixon Entrance
